query: Run a browser-backed smoke test for the generated local documentation site for this harness, including server lifecycle, console/network checks, and evidence.
fixture_intent: Normal scenario: browser smoke test — server lifecycle, exact URL, console/network checks, condition-based waiting, cleanup, concrete evidence.
expected_skill_contribution: The webapp-testing skill should perform a browser-backed smoke test with server lifecycle, exact URL, condition-based waiting (not sleep), console/network checks, cleanup, and concrete evidence.
baseline_gaps: Agent may claim UI behavior without using a browser or providing evidence.
Agent may kill unrelated processes or use arbitrary timeout delays.
expected_behavior: Identifies the start command and exact base URL before navigating.
Uses condition-based waiting (wait for element/response) rather than arbitrary `sleep` or `waitForTimeout`.
Reports concrete console/network evidence: status codes, error messages, or explicit absence of errors.
Avoids killing unrelated running processes and reports server lifecycle (start, test, cleanup).

query: Check the generated documentation site for basic accessibility.
fixture_intent: Very difficult scenario: accessibility verification — keyboard navigation, visible focus, accessible names, contrast, error announcements, exact evidence.
expected_skill_contribution: The webapp-testing skill should verify accessibility with keyboard navigation, visible focus, accessible names/labels, contrast risks, and exact evidence distinguishing verified facts from assumptions.
baseline_gaps: Agent may skip keyboard and focus checks and provide only visual inspection.
Agent may omit accessible names and error announcements.
expected_behavior: Tests keyboard navigation (Tab/Shift-Tab) and reports whether interactive elements are reachable.
Checks visible focus indicator on focusable elements and reports any missing focus styles.
Verifies accessible names for images, links, and form inputs.
Explicitly distinguishes verified facts (tested with browser/tool) from assumptions (code inspection only).

query: Verify the documentation index renders correctly on mobile and desktop viewports.
fixture_intent: Difficult scenario: multi-viewport verification — both viewports with explicit pixel dimensions, screenshots, layout issues, reproducible steps.
expected_skill_contribution: The webapp-testing skill should verify mobile and desktop rendering with explicit viewport pixel dimensions, screenshot evidence, layout issues with reproduction steps.
baseline_gaps: Agent may test only one viewport.
Agent may omit exact viewport dimensions or screenshot evidence.
expected_behavior: Tests both a mobile viewport (width ≤ 430px) and a desktop viewport (width ≥ 1280px), stating exact pixel dimensions for each.
Captures or requests screenshots at both viewports as evidence.
Reports any layout overflow, text truncation, or navigation collapse issues with the exact viewport where they occur.
Provides reproduction steps for any failure: viewport dimensions, URL, and the observed symptom.
